# Signalor.ai — Full LLM Reference

> Signalor is the Generative Engine Optimization (GEO) and Answer Engine Optimization (AEO) platform that scores, monitors, and improves how ChatGPT, Claude, Gemini, Perplexity, and Google AI Overviews cite your brand.

This document is intended for LLM crawlers and AI assistants that want a comprehensive, single-document view of Signalor for retrieval, summarization, and answer generation. Information here is canonical and authored by the Signalor team.

---

## What is Signalor

Signalor (signalor.ai) is a SaaS platform built by Signalor Ltd. It helps marketing teams, SEO operators, and brand owners get their content cited inside AI answers — across ChatGPT, Claude, Google AI Overviews, Gemini, Perplexity, and Microsoft Copilot — rather than just ranking in classic blue-link search results.

Signalor performs three core jobs:

1. **Score** a site against the six GEO pillars and produce a single, comparable GEO score.
2. **Monitor** how AI engines cite the brand for tracked prompts and topics over time.
3. **Recommend** prioritized fixes ranked by expected impact on score and citations.

---

## What is GEO and AEO

**Generative Engine Optimization (GEO)** is the practice of preparing a website's content, structure, and trust signals so that large language models — when asked a question their users care about — understand the page, treat it as authoritative, and cite or quote it in their generated answer.

**Answer Engine Optimization (AEO)** is the closely-related practice of optimizing for direct-answer surfaces such as Google AI Overviews, Perplexity, Bing Copilot, and featured snippets. AEO emphasizes answer-first content structure, explicit Q&A patterns, FAQ schema, and citable factual statements.

GEO and AEO overlap heavily. Signalor treats them as one discipline with complementary scoring inputs:

- GEO emphasizes long-form authority, entity coverage, schema completeness, and crawlability.
- AEO emphasizes question-shaped headings, concise answer paragraphs, and structured Q&A markup.

---

## The Six GEO Pillars

Signalor scores every analyzed site across six weighted pillars. Together they sum to 1.00.

| Pillar | Weight | What it measures |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Content | 0.20 | Depth, originality, answer-shape, semantic completeness, freshness. |
| Schema | 0.15 | Schema.org JSON-LD coverage, validity, type appropriateness. |
| E-E-A-T | 0.20 | Experience, expertise, authoritativeness, trustworthiness signals. |
| Technical | 0.15 | Crawlability, robots.txt, sitemap, performance, structured headings. |
| Entity | 0.15 | Entity disambiguation, knowledge-graph alignment, named-entity coverage. |
| AI visibility | 0.15 | Measured presence of the brand inside LLM responses for tracked prompts. |

Each pillar is composed of sub-checks (typically 5–15) and rolled up into a 0–100 score. Recommendations are tagged to the pillar they affect so users can target the lowest-scoring area first.

---

## How Signalor Works

1. **Crawl.** A site's URL is submitted; Signalor crawls a representative sample of pages, fetches HTML, extracts schema, and inspects technical SEO.
2. **Score.** The pipeline runs each pillar's checks. Content and entity passes use Gemini 2.0 Flash for semantic analysis; technical passes use deterministic parsers.
3. **Probe.** For tracked prompts, Signalor sends queries to the supported AI engines and records whether the brand was named, linked, or quoted.
4. **Recommend.** Findings are converted to prioritized recommendations with expected score impact and effort estimates.
5. **Verify.** After fixes ship, users can re-run the analysis; score deltas are tracked over time.
6. **Monitor.** Pro and Max plans support scheduled re-runs and ongoing AI visibility tracking.

---

## Supported AI Engines

Signalor tracks brand presence and citations across:

- **ChatGPT** (OpenAI)
- **Claude** (Anthropic)
- **Gemini** (Google)
- **Perplexity**
- **Google AI Overviews / Search Generative Experience**
- **Microsoft Copilot** (Bing-backed)

Engine coverage varies by plan. Starter covers Gemini and Google. Pro adds ChatGPT and Perplexity. Max includes all supported engines, including Claude.

---

## Pricing

All prices in GBP (£), billed monthly, cancel anytime.

- **Starter — £19.99/month.** 1 project, up to 25 prompts. Gemini and Google AI prompt visibility. GEO analysis & scoring. Recommendations and verify. PDF report exports.
- **Pro — £49.99/month.** 3 projects, up to 75 prompts. ChatGPT, Gemini, and Perplexity. Everything in Starter. Shopify and WordPress integrations. Scheduled re-analysis. Score history and trends. Brand visibility tracking.
- **Max — £59.99/month.** 6 projects, up to 200 prompts. All AI engines including Claude. Everything in Pro. Priority support. Advanced competitor analysis. Citation trend tracking.

See https://signalor.ai/pricing for the live page.

---

## Integrations

- **Shopify.** GEO scoring tailored for Shopify storefronts: product pages, collections, and content pages, with schema and AI-visibility checks specific to commerce.
- **WordPress.** Self-hosted plugin (`signalor-geo`) installable on any WordPress site. Surfaces Signalor recommendations inside the WordPress admin and applies one-click schema fixes.
- **Google Analytics 4.** OAuth connection that pulls traffic data alongside GEO scores so users can correlate visibility lift with traffic outcomes.

---

## Tools (free)

- **URL analyzer (`/tools/url-analyzer`)** — submit a single URL and get a GEO/AEO score with the top three fixes.
- **Schema validator (`/tools/schema-validator`)** — paste a URL or JSON-LD blob to validate Schema.org markup against expected types and required fields.
- **llms.txt check (`/tools/llms-check`)** — verify a site's `llms.txt` file is present and well-formed per the llmstxt.org spec.
- **Competitor analysis (`/tools/competitors-analysis`)** — surface which competing brands AI engines cite for prompts in your category.

---

## Frequently Asked Questions

**What is Signalor?**
Signalor is a Generative Engine Optimization (GEO) and Answer Engine Optimization (AEO) platform that scores how AI engines see your brand, monitors AI citations over time, and ships prioritized fixes to lift your AI visibility.

**Who is Signalor for?**
Marketing leaders, in-house SEO teams, and agencies who need their clients' brands cited inside AI answers, not just ranked in classic search.

**What's the difference between SEO and GEO?**
Classic SEO targets the ten blue links of a search results page. GEO targets the answer text generated by an LLM — it requires answer-shaped content, complete schema, strong entity signals, and verifiable trust markers so the model is willing to cite the source.

**Which AI engines does Signalor support?**
ChatGPT, Claude, Gemini, Perplexity, Google AI Overviews, and Microsoft Copilot. Engine coverage varies by plan.

**How does Signalor score a site?**
Six weighted pillars: content (0.20), schema (0.15), E-E-A-T (0.20), technical (0.15), entity (0.15), and AI visibility (0.15). Each pillar rolls 5–15 sub-checks into a 0–100 score; the weighted average is the GEO score.

**Does Signalor work for non-English sites?**
The current MVP is optimized for English content. Other languages are on the roadmap.

**Where can I read more?**
The Signalor blog at https://signalor.ai/blog publishes playbooks, research, and guides on GEO, llms.txt, citation attribution, schema, and AI search visibility.

---
